{{Infobox Kofun | Name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other Name = | Belongs to = Miyake Kofun Cluster | Location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | Type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | Size = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in **the central Nara Basin**, on slightly elevated land between the **Teragawa and Asuka rivers**. It is positioned at the **southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster**, with the **Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")** passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is **keyhole-shaped** and faces **west-southwest**. It consists of **two tiers**.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - **No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)** was found, but a row of **cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)**, including **"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa**, was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The **front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side**.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a **moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep**,{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an **overall length of 86 meters** (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - **Haniwa (埴輪)** - **Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts** - **Green jasper tubular beads** - **Soapstone double-hole disk** The **burial facility has not been excavated**, but it is suspected to be a **horizontal stone chamber**.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == **Construction Period** == - Estimated to be **early 6th century** (**Late Kofun period**). - **One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin**. - Some scholars theorize that the **Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)** system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == **Excavation History** == - **1983 (Showa 58):** Designated as a **Nara Prefectural Historic Site**.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - **1983 (1st Survey):** Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - **1984 (2nd Survey):** Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - **1986 (3rd Survey):** Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - **1996 (4th Survey):** Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - **2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):** Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == **Mound Structure** == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - **Mound size:** - **Total length:** 70m - **Rear circular section:** Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m - **Front rectangular section:** Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == **Cultural Heritage Status** == === **Nara Prefectural Cultural Property** === - **Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun** (Designated **March 15, 1983**). == **References** == {{Reflist}} == **Bibliography** == {{Refbegin}} * **Historical Site Information Board** (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == **Related Sites** == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮}} == **External Links** == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. {{Infobox Kofun | Name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other Name = | Belongs to = Miyake Kofun Cluster | Location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | Type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | Size = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in **the central Nara Basin**, on slightly elevated land between the **Teragawa and Asuka rivers**. It is positioned at the **southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster**, with the **Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")** passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is **keyhole-shaped** and faces **west-southwest**. It consists of **two tiers**.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - **No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)** was found, but a row of **cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)**, including **"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa**, was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The **front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side**.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a **moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep**,{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an **overall length of 86 meters** (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - **Haniwa (埴輪)** - **Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts** - **Green jasper tubular beads** - **Soapstone double-hole disk** The **burial facility has not been excavated**, but it is suspected to be a **horizontal stone chamber**.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == **Construction Period** == - Estimated to be **early 6th century** (**Late Kofun period**). - **One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin**. - Some scholars theorize that the **Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)** system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == **Excavation History** == - **1983 (Showa 58):** Designated as a **Nara Prefectural Historic Site**.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - **1983 (1st Survey):** Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - **1984 (2nd Survey):** Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - **1986 (3rd Survey):** Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - **1996 (4th Survey):** Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - **2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):** Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == **Mound Structure** == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - **Mound size:** - **Total length:** 70m - **Rear circular section:** Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m - **Front rectangular section:** Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == **Cultural Heritage Status** == === **Nara Prefectural Cultural Property** === - **Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun** (Designated **March 15, 1983**). == **References** == {{Reflist}} == **Bibliography** == {{Refbegin}} * **Historical Site Information Board** (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == **Related Sites** == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮}} == **External Links** == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] {{Infobox Kofun | Name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other Name = | Belongs to = Miyake Kofun Cluster | Location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | Type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | Size = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' - '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts''' - '''Green jasper tubular beads''' - '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''Construction Period''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). - '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == - '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound Structure''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - '''Mound size:''' - '''Total length:''' 70m - '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m - '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact

Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' - '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts''' - '''Green jasper tubular beads''' - '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''Construction Period''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). - '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == - '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound Structure''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - '''Mound size:''' - '''Total length:''' 70m - '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m - '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact

{{Infobox Kofun | Name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other Name = | Belongs to = Miyake Kofun Cluster | Location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | Type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | Size = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' - '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts''' - '''Green jasper tubular beads''' - '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''Construction Period''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). - '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == - '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound Structure''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - '''Mound size:''' - '''Total length:''' 70m - '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m - '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''Cultural Heritage Status''' == === '''Nara Prefectural Cultural Property''' === - '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historical Site Information Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] {{Infobox Kofun | Name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other Name = | Belongs to = Miyake Kofun Cluster | Location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | Type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | Size = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' - '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts''' - '''Green jasper tubular beads''' - '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''Construction Period''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). - '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == - '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound Structure''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''Cultural Heritage Status''' == === '''Nara Prefectural Cultural Property''' === - '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historical Site Information Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] {{Infobox Kofun | Name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other Name = | Belongs to = Miyake Kofun Cluster | Location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | Type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | Size = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' - '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts''' - '''Green jasper tubular beads''' - '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''Construction Period''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). - '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == - '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound Structure''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact

Tumulus (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other Name = | Belongs to = Miyake Kofun Cluster | Location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | Type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | Size = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' - '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts''' - '''Green jasper tubular beads''' - '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''Construction Period''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). - '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == - '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound Structure''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact

It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. {{Infobox Kofun | Name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other Name = | Belongs to = Miyake Kofun Cluster | Location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | Type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | Size = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' - '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts''' - '''Green jasper tubular beads''' - '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''Construction Period''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). - '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == - '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound Structure''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''Cultural Heritage Status''' == === '''Nara Prefectural Cultural Property''' === - '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historical Site Information Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] Tumulus (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other Name = | Belongs to = Miyake Kofun Cluster | Location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | Type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | Size = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' - '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts''' - '''Green jasper tubular beads''' - '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''Construction Period''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). - '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == - '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound Structure''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''Cultural Heritage Status''' == === '''Nara Prefectural Cultural Property''' === - '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historical Site Information Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | Name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other Name = | Belongs to = Miyake Kofun Cluster | Location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | Type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | Size = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' - '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts''' - '''Green jasper tubular beads''' - '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''Construction Period''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). - '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == - '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound Structure''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''Cultural Heritage Status''' == === '''Nara Prefectural Cultural Property''' === - '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historical Site Information Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' - '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts''' - '''Green jasper tubular beads''' - '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''Construction Period''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). - '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == - '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - {{Infobox Kofun | Name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other Name = | Belongs to = Miyake Kofun Cluster | Location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | Type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | Size = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | Burial Facility = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | Excavated Artifacts = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden artifacts | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Early [[6th century]] | Buried Individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Cultural Properties = | Notable Points = | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been designated as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historical Site Information Board. - '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several artifacts have been discovered: - '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' - '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden artifacts''' - '''Green jasper tubular beads''' - '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial artifacts have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''Construction Period''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). - '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == - '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|format=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural Cultural Property List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} - '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). - '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). - '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). - '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). - '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound Structure''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}}]] - '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
