Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historic Site Intypeation Board. * '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several burial goods have been discovered: * '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' * '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden burial goods''' * '''Green jasper tubular beads''' * '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial burial goods have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''built''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). * '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == * '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|typeat=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural designation1 List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} * '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). * '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). * '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). * '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). * '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound burial''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}]]] * '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''designation1''' == === '''Nara Prefectural designation1''' === * '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historic Site Intypeation Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮|12=draft}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{citation needed|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} {{disambiguation]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completed]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[sn:uroda Ōtsuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other name = | part_of = Miyake Kofun Cluster | location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | area = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | burial = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Early [[6th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Historic Site = | Notable Points = | map = Japan Nara | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | map_dot_label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been Historic Site1 as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historic Site Intypeation Board. * '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several burial goods have been discovered: * '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' * '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden burial goods''' * '''Green jasper tubular beads''' * '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial burial goods have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''built''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). * '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == * '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|typeat=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural designation1 List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} * '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). * '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). * '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). * '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). * '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound burial''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}]]] * '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''designation1''' == === '''Nara Prefectural designation1''' === * '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historic Site Intypeation Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮|12=draft}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{citation needed|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} {{disambiguation]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completed]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[sn:uroda Ōtsuka Kofun]]

From within the moat, several burial goods have been discovered: * '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' * '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden burial goods''' * '''Green jasper tubular beads''' * '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial burial goods have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''built''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). * '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == * '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|typeat=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural designation1 List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} * '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). * '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). * '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). * '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). * '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound burial''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}]]] * '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''designation1''' == === '''Nara Prefectural designation1''' === * '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historic Site Intypeation Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮|12=draft}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{citation needed|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} {{disambiguation]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completed]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[sn:uroda Ōtsuka Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other name = | part_of = Miyake Kofun Cluster | location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | area = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | burial = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Early [[6th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Historic Site = | Notable Points = | map = Japan Nara | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | map_dot_label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been Historic Site1 as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historic Site Intypeation Board. * '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several burial goods have been discovered: * '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' * '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden burial goods''' * '''Green jasper tubular beads''' * '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial burial goods have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''built''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). * '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == * '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|typeat=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural designation1 List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} * '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). * '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). * '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). * '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). * '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound burial''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}]]] * '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''designation1''' == === '''Nara Prefectural designation1''' === * '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historic Site Intypeation Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮|12=draft}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{citation needed|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} {{disambiguation]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completed]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[sn:uroda Ōtsuka Kofun]]

Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). * '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == * '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|typeat=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural designation1 List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} * '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). * '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). * '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). * '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). * '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound burial''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}]]] * '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact

Kofun (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other name = | part_of = Miyake Kofun Cluster | location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | area = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | burial = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Early [[6th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Historic Site = | Notable Points = | map = Japan Nara | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | map_dot_label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been Historic Site1 as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historic Site Intypeation Board. * '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several burial goods have been discovered: * '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' * '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden burial goods''' * '''Green jasper tubular beads''' * '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial burial goods have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''built''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). * '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == * '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|typeat=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural designation1 List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} * '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). * '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). * '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). * '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). * '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound burial''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}]]] * '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''designation1''' == === '''Nara Prefectural designation1''' === * '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historic Site Intypeation Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮|12=draft}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{citation needed|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Kofun (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other name = | part_of = Miyake Kofun Cluster | location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | area = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | burial = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Early [[6th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Historic Site = | Notable Points = | map = Japan Nara | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | map_dot_label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been Historic Site1 as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historic Site Intypeation Board. * '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several burial goods have been discovered: * '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' * '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden burial goods''' * '''Green jasper tubular beads''' * '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial burial goods have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''built''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). * '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == * '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|typeat=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural designation1 List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} * '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). * '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). * '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). * '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). * '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound burial''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}]]] * '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}} {{disambiguation]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completed]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[sn:uroda Ōtsuka Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other name = | part_of = Miyake Kofun Cluster | location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | area = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | burial = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Early [[6th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Historic Site = | Notable Points = | map = Japan Nara | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | map_dot_label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been Historic Site1 as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historic Site Intypeation Board. * '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several burial goods have been discovered: * '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' * '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden burial goods''' * '''Green jasper tubular beads''' * '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial burial goods have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''built''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). * '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == * '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|typeat=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural designation1 List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} * '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). * '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). * '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). * '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). * '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound burial''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}]]] * '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''designation1''' == === '''Nara Prefectural designation1''' === * '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historic Site Intypeation Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{citation needed|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}} {{disambiguation]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completed]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[sn:uroda Ōtsuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:黒田大塚古墳 up to rev 100400277
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 黒田大塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に後円部、左奥に前方部） |別名 = |所属 = 三宅古墳群 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]大字黒田字東藪 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 34|緯度秒 = 5.00 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 34.15 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長70m
高さ8.2m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 管玉・双孔円板・[[埴輪]]・木製品 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 奈良県指定史跡「黒田大塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 |ラベル = 黒田大塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{座標一覧}} '''黒田大塚古墳'''（くろだおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]黒田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。三宅古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]中央部、寺川・飛鳥川に挟まれた微高地に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。三宅古墳群では南端に位置し、東側には古道である筋違道（太子道）が通じる{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。これまでに数次の調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西南西方向に向ける。墳丘は2段築成[[#参考文献]] 史跡説明板より。。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]は認められないが{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}、[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形・蓋形埴輪含む）が認められる（周濠内から出土）{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘の前方部北側には張り出し部が認められる{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされており、幅8メートル・深さ0.8メートルを測り{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}、周濠を含めた古墳全長は86メートルにおよぶ。周濠内からは埴輪のほか、鳥形・蓋形（笠形）木製品、碧玉製管玉、滑石製双孔円板が検出されている。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでなく（[[横穴式石室]]の可能性{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}）、副葬品も詳らかでない{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。奈良盆地中央部では数少ない後期大型前方後円墳として注目され、一帯の三宅古墳群は中小規模の前方後円墳・[[円墳]]・[[方墳]]から構成されることから、[[屯倉]]に関わる古墳群とする説が挙げられている{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 古墳域は[[1983年]]（[[昭和]]58年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1983年]]（[[昭和]]58年）3月15日、奈良県指定史跡に指定。 * 1983年度（昭和58年度）、第1次調査（田原本町教育委員会、[[1984年]]に概要報告）。 * 1984年度（昭和59年度）、第2次調査（田原本町教育委員会、[[1986年]]に概要報告）。 * 1986年度（昭和61年度）、第3次調査（田原本町教育委員会、[[1987年]]に概要報告）。 * [[1996年]]度（[[平成]]8年度）、第4次調査（田原本町教育委員会、[[1997年]]に概要報告）。 * [[2003年]]度（平成15年度）、第5・6次調査（田原本町教育委員会、[[2004年]]に概要報告）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|右に前方部、左に後円部}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 墳丘長：70メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：40メートル ** 高さ：8.2メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：45メートル ** 高さ：7.7メートル Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|円筒埴輪・蓋形木製品
{{small|唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 黒田大塚古墳 - 1983年（昭和58年）3月15日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf |format=PDF |title=奈良県指定文化財一覧 |website=奈良県ホームページ |publisher=[[奈良県]] |date= |accessdate=2021-03-07 }}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} {{Refbegin}} * 史跡説明板（奈良県教育委員会、1995年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|author=河上邦彦|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|黒田大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} {{Refbegin}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/940 昭和58年度 唐古・鍵遺跡 -第16・18・19次発掘調査概報- 黒田大塚古墳 -第1次発掘調査概報-（田原本町埋蔵文化財調査概要2）]|publisher=田原本町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/972 昭和59年度 唐古・鍵遺跡 -第20次発掘調査概報- 黒田大塚古墳 -第2次発掘調査概報-（田原本町埋蔵文化財調査概要3）]|publisher=田原本町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/845 昭和61年度 唐古・鍵遺跡 -第27・28次発掘調査概報- 黒田大塚古墳 -第3次発掘調査概報-（田原本町埋蔵文化財調査概要8）]|publisher=田原本町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=黒田大塚古墳 第4次調査|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1642 田原本町埋蔵文化財調査年報6 -1996年度-]|publisher=田原本町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=黒田大塚古墳 第5次調査・黒田大塚古墳 第6次調査|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1746 田原本町埋蔵文化財調査年報13 -2003年度-]|publisher=田原本町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 {{Refend}} == 関連項目 == * [[庵戸宮]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html 黒田大塚古墳] - 田原本町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:くろたおおつかこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[黒田大塚古墳]] to [[Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 黒田大塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に後円部、左奥に前方部） |別名 = |所属 = 三宅古墳群 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]大字黒田字東藪 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 34|緯度秒 = 5.00 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 34.15 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長70m
高さ8.2m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 管玉・双孔円板・[[埴輪]]・木製品 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 奈良県指定史跡「黒田大塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 |ラベル = 黒田大塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{座標一覧}} '''黒田大塚古墳'''（くろだおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]黒田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。三宅古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]中央部、寺川・飛鳥川に挟まれた微高地に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。三宅古墳群では南端に位置し、東側には古道である筋違道（太子道）が通じる{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。これまでに数次の調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西南西方向に向ける。墳丘は2段築成[[#参考文献]] 史跡説明板より。。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]は認められないが{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}、[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形・蓋形埴輪含む）が認められる（周濠内から出土）{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘の前方部北側には張り出し部が認められる{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされており、幅8メートル・深さ0.8メートルを測り{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}、周濠を含めた古墳全長は86メートルにおよぶ。周濠内からは埴輪のほか、鳥形・蓋形（笠形）木製品、碧玉製管玉、滑石製双孔円板が検出されている。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでなく（[[横穴式石室]]の可能性{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}）、副葬品も詳らかでない{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。奈良盆地中央部では数少ない後期大型前方後円墳として注目され、一帯の三宅古墳群は中小規模の前方後円墳・[[円墳]]・[[方墳]]から構成されることから、[[屯倉]]に関わる古墳群とする説が挙げられている{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 古墳域は[[1983年]]（[[昭和]]58年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1983年]]（[[昭和]]58年）3月15日、奈良県指定史跡に指定。 * 1983年度（昭和58年度）、第1次調査（田原本町教育委員会、[[1984年]]に概要報告）。 * 1984年度（昭和59年度）、第2次調査（田原本町教育委員会、[[1986年]]に概要報告）。 * 1986年度（昭和61年度）、第3次調査（田原本町教育委員会、[[1987年]]に概要報告）。 * [[1996年]]度（[[平成]]8年度）、第4次調査（田原本町教育委員会、[[1997年]]に概要報告）。 * [[2003年]]度（平成15年度）、第5・6次調査（田原本町教育委員会、[[2004年]]に概要報告）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|右に前方部、左に後円部}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 墳丘長：70メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：40メートル ** 高さ：8.2メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：45メートル ** 高さ：7.7メートル Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|円筒埴輪・蓋形木製品
{{small|唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 黒田大塚古墳 - 1983年（昭和58年）3月15日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf |format=PDF |title=奈良県指定文化財一覧 |website=奈良県ホームページ |publisher=[[奈良県]] |date= |accessdate=2021-03-07 }}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} {{Refbegin}} * 史跡説明板（奈良県教育委員会、1995年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|黒田大塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|author=河上邦彦|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|黒田大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} {{Refbegin}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/940 昭和58年度 唐古・鍵遺跡 -第16・18・19次発掘調査概報- 黒田大塚古墳 -第1次発掘調査概報-（田原本町埋蔵文化財調査概要2）]|publisher=田原本町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/972 昭和59年度 唐古・鍵遺跡 -第20次発掘調査概報- 黒田大塚古墳 -第2次発掘調査概報-（田原本町埋蔵文化財調査概要3）]|publisher=田原本町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/845 昭和61年度 唐古・鍵遺跡 -第27・28次発掘調査概報- 黒田大塚古墳 -第3次発掘調査概報-（田原本町埋蔵文化財調査概要8）]|publisher=田原本町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=黒田大塚古墳 第4次調査|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1642 田原本町埋蔵文化財調査年報6 -1996年度-]|publisher=田原本町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=黒田大塚古墳 第5次調査・黒田大塚古墳 第6次調査|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1746 田原本町埋蔵文化財調査年報13 -2003年度-]|publisher=田原本町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 {{Refend}} == 関連項目 == * [[庵戸宮]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html 黒田大塚古墳] - 田原本町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:くろたおおつかこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (right: rear circular section, left back: front section) | Other name = | part_of = Miyake Kofun Cluster | location = [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|5.00|N|135|46|34.15|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | area = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | burial = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Early [[6th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Historic Site = | Notable Points = | map = Japan Nara | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | map_dot_label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been Historic Site1 as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historic Site Intypeation Board. * '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several burial goods have been discovered: * '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' * '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden burial goods''' * '''Green jasper tubular beads''' * '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial burial goods have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''built''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). * '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == * '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|typeat=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural designation1 List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} * '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). * '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). * '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). * '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). * '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound burial''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}]]] * '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == '''designation1''' == === '''Nara Prefectural designation1''' === * '''Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (Designated '''March 15, 1983'''). == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''Bibliography''' == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historic Site Intypeation Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == '''Related Sites''' == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] {{citation needed|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}} {{disambiguation]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Tawaramoto Town History]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completed]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[sn:uroda Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|黒田大塚古墳|version=100400277|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | burial = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Early [[6th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Historic Site = | Notable Points = | map = Japan Nara | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | map_dot_label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{coordinates list}} '''Kuroda Otsuka Kofun''' (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], [[Tawaramoto, Nara|Tawaramoto Town]], [[Shiki District, Nara|Shiki District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been Historic Site1 as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in '''the central Nara Basin''', on slightly elevated land between the '''Teragawa and Asuka rivers'''. It is positioned at the '''southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster''', with the '''Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road")''' passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is '''keyhole-shaped''' and faces '''west-southwest'''. It consists of '''two tiers'''.See reference section: Historic Site Intypeation Board. * '''No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving)''' was found, but a row of '''cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪)''', including '''"morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa''', was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The '''front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a '''moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep''',{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an '''overall length of 86 meters''' (including the moat). From within the moat, several burial goods have been discovered: * '''Haniwa (埴輪)''' * '''Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden burial goods''' * '''Green jasper tubular beads''' * '''Soapstone double-hole disk''' The '''burial facility has not been excavated''', but it is suspected to be a '''horizontal stone chamber'''.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial burial goods have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == '''built''' == - Estimated to be '''early 6th century''' ('''Late Kofun period'''). * '''One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin'''. - Some scholars theorize that the '''Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage)''' system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == '''Excavation History''' == * '''1983 (Showa 58):''' Designated as a '''Nara Prefectural Historic Site'''.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|typeat=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural designation1 List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} * '''1983 (1st Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). * '''1984 (2nd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). * '''1986 (3rd Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). * '''1996 (4th Survey):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). * '''2003 (5th & 6th Surveys):''' Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == '''Mound burial''' == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}]]] * '''Mound size:''' ** '''Total length:''' 70m ** '''Rear circular section:''' Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** '''Front rectangular section:''' Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}.
