Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒田大塚古墳|version=100400277|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q106211282}} [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tawaramoto]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | image = File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | Other name = | part_of = Miyake Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Tawaramoto|qid=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.56805555555555|N|135.77615277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | area = 70 meters in length
Height: 8.2m (rear circular section) | burial = (Estimated) [[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (横穴式石室)|Horizontal stone chamber]] | burial goods = Tubular beads, double-hole disk, [[Haniwa (埴輪)|haniwa]], wooden burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | Mausoleum Status = | Historical Status = [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site|"Kuroda Otsuka Kofun" (Nara Prefecture Historic Site)]] | Historic Site = | Notable Points = | map = Japan Nara | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun) | map_dot_label = Kuroda Otsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} {{coordinates list}} Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (黒田大塚古墳, くろだおおつかこふん) is a [[Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)|keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in [[Kuroda, Tawaramoto, Shiki District, Nara Prefecture|Kuroda]], {{ill|Tawaramoto, Nara|en|Tawaramoto, Nara|qid=Q1203159|lt=Tawaramoto Town|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|Shiki District, Nara|en|Shiki District, Nara|qid=Q1155036|lt=Shiki District|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the kofun in the [[Miyake Kofun Cluster (三宅古墳群)]], and it has been Historic Site1 as a [[Nara Prefectural Historic Site]]. == Overview == The kofun is located in the central Nara Basin, on slightly elevated land between the Teragawa and Asuka rivers. It is positioned at the southernmost end of the Miyake Kofun Cluster, with the Suzuchigai Road (an ancient route, also called "Prince’s Road") passing to the east. Multiple excavations have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped and faces west-southwest. It consists of two tiers.See reference section: Historic Site Intypeation Board. * No fukiishi (葺石, stone paving) was found, but a row of cylindrical haniwa (円筒埴輪), including "morning glory" and "lid-shaped" haniwa, was discovered inside the surrounding moat.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The front section has a slight protrusion on its northern side.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} - The kofun is surrounded by a moat measuring 8 meters wide and 0.8 meters deep,{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} with an overall length of 86 meters (including the moat). From within the moat, several burial goods have been discovered: * Haniwa (埴輪) * Bird-shaped and lid-shaped (笠形) wooden burial goods * Green jasper tubular beads * Soapstone double-hole disk The burial facility has not been excavated, but it is suspected to be a horizontal stone chamber.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} No detailed burial burial goods have been confirmed.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|2006}} == built == - Estimated to be early 6th century (Late Kofun period). * One of the few large late-period keyhole-shaped tombs in the Nara Basin. - Some scholars theorize that the Miyake Kofun Cluster is related to the "Tonomikura" (屯倉, imperial rice storage) system.{{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}} == Excavation History == * 1983 (Showa 58): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf|typeat=PDF|title=Nara Prefectural designation1 List|website=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-07}} * 1983 (1st Survey): Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1984). * 1984 (2nd Survey): Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1986). * 1986 (3rd Survey): Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1987). * 1996 (4th Survey): Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 1997). * 2003 (5th & 6th Surveys): Conducted by Tawaramoto Town Board of Education (summary reported in 2004). == Mound burial == [[File:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Right: Front section, Left: Rear section}]]] * Mound size: ** Total length: 70m ** Rear circular section: Diameter 40m, Height 8.2m ** Front rectangular section: Width 45m, Height 7.7m Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Kuroda Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front 黒田大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・笠形木製品.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and lid-shaped wooden artifact
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition at the 唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム (Karako-Kagi Archaeology Museum).}} == designation1 == === Nara Prefectural designation1 === * Historic Site: Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Designated March 15, 1983). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{Refbegin}} * '''Historic Site Intypeation Board''' (奈良県教育委員会, Installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系)]] 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Heibonsha Edition)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Kunihiko Kawakami (河上邦彦)|authorlink=Kunihiko Kawakami|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 黒田大塚古墳|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten (日本古墳大辞典)]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan (東京堂出版)]]|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{Harvid|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun (Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|1989}}}} {{Refend}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Ioto Palace|ja|庵戸宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ioto Palace|1=Ioto Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486233}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/iseki/5177.html Kuroda Otsuka Kofun - Tawaramoto Town Official Site] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|黒田大塚古墳|version=100400277|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kuroda Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q106211282|co|commons:Category:Kuroda Otsuka Kofun|ja|黒田大塚古墳|ja|磯城郡田原本町|ja|牟佐大塚古墳|ja|岡山市|ja|箭田大塚古墳|ja|倉敷市|ja|今宿大塚古墳|ja|福岡市西区|ja|今宿古墳群|ja|田主丸大塚古墳|ja|浮羽郡田主丸町|ja|田主丸古墳群|ja|大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)|ja|大塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tawaramoto]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
